failure_modes: Targets move before contact because of initial velocity leakage.
Targets move before contact because colliders overlap at t=0.
LLM scale is interpreted as radius in one stage and diameter in another.
A visually good render is accepted without trajectory causality evidence.
iteration_moves: Inspect physics.json for passive initial velocities.
Inspect first trajectory frames for target speed before first active contact.
Adjust radius/extent and initial spacing before increasing speed.
Only then sweep driver speed, restitution, and friction to tune spread.
prompt_moves: Express one active striker and a passive target group.
Let the LLM infer reasonable mass, radius, friction, and restitution, but keep passive initial velocity at zero.
Use speed wording such as slow, medium, or fast as control intent, not as a template selection.
runtime_contract: Cue or driver body may have initial velocity or impulse.
Passive target bodies start with zero linear and angular velocity.
Collision geometry must not overlap at t=0; small spheres keep their explicit radius instead of being clamped larger.
Trajectory and contact events are the only source of post-contact target motion.

Post-contact target displacement or spread matches requested intensity.

capabilities.description: Compile falling or stacking prompts into gravity-driven rigid-body traces where objects descend under gravity and collide with a support surface instead of being visually animated.

failure_modes: Object is moved downward by visual keyframes without physics evidence.
Gravity or collision is disabled on a falling body.
Trajectory lacks support contact or passes through the floor.
iteration_moves: Fix initial height and support plane before changing material properties.
Check trajectory z-series and contact pairs.
Tune mass, restitution, damping, and solver substeps after gravity/contact validity passes.

Infer gravity, mass, collision shape, and collision enabled state explicitly.
Avoid visual-only downward animation as a substitute for runtime trajectory evidence.
runtime_contract: Falling bodies start above support surfaces with gravity and collision enabled.
Runtime trajectory shows decreasing height before contact.
Contact events include ground, floor, support, or another rigid body.
capabilities.title: Rigid Body Gravity Collision
verifier_checks: Gravity is enabled and non-zero.
Falling body collision is enabled.
Trajectory z decreases before first support contact.
Ground/support contact is recorded.

capabilities.description: Compile domino or chain-reaction prompts into ordered contact propagation where only the first body is actively triggered and later bodies move after upstream contact.

| domino chain | `sequential_contact_propagation` | first object is actively triggered, later objects tip through ordered adjacent contacts |

failure_modes: Later chain objects are pre-animated or pre-rotated.
Contact pairs are missing even though objects appear to fall.
Tip order is reversed or simultaneous beyond tolerance.
iteration_moves: Check initial angular velocity on all non-first objects.
Inspect adjacent contact pair coverage.
Adjust spacing, mass, friction, and angular damping after sequence validity passes.
prompt_moves: Define an ordered chain with stable ids.
Allow only the first object to receive active trigger motion.
Require later objects to remain passive until contact propagation reaches them.
runtime_contract: First chain object may have impulse or angular velocity.
Subsequent chain objects start with zero linear and angular velocity.
Trajectory records increasing tip/motion start times and adjacent contact pairs.
capabilities.title: Sequential Contact Propagation
verifier_checks: Non-first objects have no initial motion.
Tip or motion start times are non-decreasing along the chain.
Adjacent contact pairs are present in order.

terms=("billiard", "billiards", "pool", "cue ball", "cue_ball", "台球", "白球", "目标球", "ball collision"),

iteration_moves: Inspect physics.json for passive initial velocities.
Inspect first trajectory frames for target speed before first active contact.

capabilities.title: Generic Rigid-Body Contact Causality
verifier_checks: Passive initial velocity is near zero.
No passive pre-contact displacement above threshold.
Expected contact graph is present.
Motion after render is explained by contact.

capabilities.description: Validate restitution-controlled rigid-body rebound after impact. The smoke family uses a ball dropping onto a support surface, but the invariant applies to any bouncing rigid body.

evidence.text: Validate restitution-controlled rigid-body rebound after impact.

iteration_moves: Inspect z trajectory before and after first support contact.
Check restitution label and rebound-height envelope before tuning material.
Reject energy gain unless an external impulse is declared.
prompt_moves: Identify bouncing body, support surface, drop height, restitution, gravity, and expected rebound envelope.
runtime_contract: Bouncing body descends under gravity before first contact.
Post-contact rebound height is bounded by restitution and initial drop height.
capabilities.title: Bounce Restitution Validation
verifier_checks: Object descends before first contact.
First impact contact exists.
Post-contact rebound height is not too low for restitution.
Post-contact rebound height does not exceed the allowed energy envelope.

capabilities.description: Validate friction-controlled rolling motion on a flat support surface. The smoke family uses a ball with initial horizontal velocity, but the invariant applies to any rolling rigid body with contact friction.

evidence.text: Validate friction-controlled rolling motion on a flat support surface.

iteration_moves: Inspect support contact events before interpreting rolling distance.
Compare initial and final horizontal speed against friction labels.
Reject too-long travel or too-short travel before tuning material properties.
prompt_moves: Identify rolling body, support surface, initial horizontal velocity, friction, and expected distance envelope.
runtime_contract: Rolling body starts with declared horizontal velocity.
Support contact is exported during rolling.
Horizontal speed decays and travel distance remains friction-bounded.
capabilities.title: Rolling Friction Validation
verifier_checks: Initial horizontal velocity is non-zero.
Support contact exists during rolling.
Horizontal speed decays.
Forward displacement is within the expected friction response envelope.

capabilities.description: Validate flat-surface sliding friction and static-friction threshold behavior. The smoke family uses a crate, but the invariant applies to any sliding rigid body with a support contact and friction material.

evidence.text: Validate flat-surface sliding friction and static-friction threshold behavior.

iteration_moves: Inspect support contact before checking sliding distance.
Compare dynamic-friction stopping distance against final speed.
For static-threshold cases, verify applied force is below static limit before expecting no motion.
prompt_moves: Identify sliding body, support surface, initial horizontal velocity or applied force, static friction, dynamic friction, and expected stop/static envelope.
runtime_contract: Sliding mode starts with declared horizontal velocity and decelerates.
Static-threshold mode declares applied force below static friction limit and remains nearly stationary.
Support contact and friction labels are exported.
capabilities.title: Sliding Crate Friction Validation
verifier_checks: Support contact exists.
Sliding body decelerates and stops within distance range.
Static-threshold body remains under displacement/speed thresholds.
Motion does not accelerate or reverse without declared external force.

capabilities.title: Prompt Case Capability Planning
capabilities.description: Map an agent task or natural-language prompt into a capability-backed case family, required evidence streams, and explicit repairable execution plan.
prompt_moves: The prompt is intent, not an executable simulation.
The selected capability should describe reusable physical invariants rather than a scene template.
Every planned object should have a stable id, role, and evidence requirement before runtime.
verifier_checks: Primary capability is not a deprecated compatibility alias.
Planning output includes pipeline stages, physics constraints, asset operations, and verification layers.
Unsupported prompts fall back to explicit physics control rather than a visual-only plan.
iteration_moves: Add prompt terms or a capability rule for the missing physical invariant.
Create a parameterized case template instead of a one-off prompt template.
Attach required signals and verifier rules before adding runtime code.

evidence.text: Map an agent task or natural-language prompt into a capability-backed case family, required evidence streams, and explicit repairable execution plan.

capabilities.title: Physics Verifier Truth Gate
capabilities.description: Use structured verifier evidence, not UI preview or successful rendering alone, as the final readiness decision for a case or dataset sample.
prompt_moves: A video can look plausible while violating physical causality.
Verifier decisions must be based on trajectory, contact events, render pass contracts, and asset binding evidence.
Every failure should map to a stable failure type and repair suggestion.
verifier_checks: Physics invariant status is pass, fail, or warning.
Render and signal readiness are separate from physics readiness.
Failure reports include first_failure, evidence, and repair_suggestions.
Expected negative cases are counted separately from runtime/preflight failures.
iteration_moves: Repair the earliest failed layer before changing downstream rendering.
Add a negative regression case for each new verifier rule.
Do not promote samples to datasets unless verifier and render sync gates pass.

evidence.text: Use structured verifier evidence, not UI preview or successful rendering alone, as the final readiness decision for a case or dataset sample.

capabilities.title: Dataset Artifact Packaging
capabilities.description: Package only verifier-gated runtime artifacts into dataset-ready sample layouts with lineage, hashes, signal availability, and failure visibility.
prompt_moves: Dataset samples must preserve the link from prompt/case to scene, assets, physics controls, runtime outputs, and verifier decision.
Failed or incomplete samples should remain auditable but not training-ready.
Generated media, local assets, and runs are local artifacts and should not be committed to the harness repo.
verifier_checks: Manifest references only existing files.
Readiness fields agree with verifier and render sync reports.
Sample source type and proxy status are explicit.
Generated artifacts are excluded from public commits.
iteration_moves: Fix missing runtime artifacts before packaging.
Keep failed samples in reports but outside training-ready shards.
Use hashes and manifest paths to detect accidental artifact drift.

evidence.text: Package only verifier-gated runtime artifacts into dataset-ready sample layouts with lineage, hashes, signal availability, and failure visibility.

capabilities.title: Force Field Wind Drift
capabilities.description: Validate wind or force-field driven drift for light rigid bodies or buoyant objects using explicit wind vector labels and trajectory displacement evidence. The balloon case is only a smoke family.
prompt_moves: Wind is a structured force-field control, not a hidden animation path.
Wind-driven objects must declare mass or buoyancy-sensitive physical properties.
Observed horizontal drift should align with the wind vector within expected displacement bounds.
verifier_checks: Expected wind vector exists and has non-zero horizontal magnitude.
Wind subject trajectory has at least two frames.
Projected displacement along wind direction stays within expected range.
Drift opposite the wind vector is rejected.
Altitude remains inside the expected envelope when specified.
iteration_moves: Move wind speed and direction into expected_physics.wind_vector_m_s or physics_control.force_field.wind.
Bind the wind subject as a physics-critical light body, not a visual-only sprite.
Use negative cases for missing wind labels, no drift, and wrong drift direction.

evidence.text: Wind or force-field driven drift requires explicit wind vector labels and trajectory displacement evidence.

capabilities.title: Mass Ratio Momentum Transfer
capabilities.description: Validate one-dimensional rigid-body momentum transfer across a contact event using explicit mass ratio, restitution, and post-collision velocity evidence. The two-body collision is only a smoke family.
prompt_moves: The moving active body transfers momentum to a passive receiver through runtime contact.
Both bodies must declare positive mass values before runtime.
Post-collision velocity ordering should match the declared mass ratio and restitution envelope.
verifier_checks: Active and passive bodies declare positive mass.
Passive body initial velocity is near zero.
Expected active-passive contact is recorded.
Post-contact target velocity is in the declared direction and bounded range.
Velocity ordering follows heavy-to-light or light-to-heavy mass ratio expectations.
Kinetic energy does not exceed the allowed energy-gain ratio.
iteration_moves: Declare mass_kg on both colliding bodies.
Remove hidden initial velocity from passive receivers.
Use restitution and mass ratio to derive expected velocity bounds instead of hard-coded target motion.

evidence.text: Mass-ratio momentum transfer requires mass labels, contact events, post-collision velocity evidence, and an energy-gain bound.

capabilities.title: Angular Damping Spin Decay
capabilities.description: Validate rotational damping for spinning rigid bodies using explicit angular velocity, angular damping labels, rotation trace, and monotonic spin-decay evidence. The spinning sphere is only a smoke family.
prompt_moves: Spin is a structured angular-velocity control, not a visual-only rotation animation.
Declare initial angular velocity, angular damping, and spin axis before runtime.
Use rotation trace and angular_velocity evidence to validate decay instead of trusting rendered motion blur.
verifier_checks: Initial angular velocity and angular damping labels are present.
Trajectory contains angular_velocity_deg_s for the spinning body.
Angular speed decays by at least the expected amount.
Final angular speed stays below the expected upper bound.
Angular speed does not increase without an external torque label.
Rotation delta is large enough to prove the body actually spun.
iteration_moves: Move spin controls into initial_angular_velocity_deg_s and expected_physics.angular_damping.
Bind spinning bodies as physics-critical rigid bodies with inertia/damping metadata.
Use negative cases for missing labels, no decay, and unexplained angular speed gain.

evidence.text: Angular damping requires explicit angular velocity labels, rotation trace, and monotonic spin-decay evidence.

capabilities.title: Agent Action To Rigid-Body Coupling
capabilities.description: Validate that agent or controller actions cause rigid-body motion through explicit action trace, contact or impulse evidence, and post-action trajectory response. Pushing a box or throwing a ball are smoke families.
prompt_moves: Represent agent intent as structured action_trace events with actor_id, target_id, action_type, frame, and time.
Keep the target rigid body still before the action frame.
Use contact evidence for push actions and impulse/release metadata for throw actions.
verifier_checks: Action trace exists and references known actor/target ids.
Target initial and pre-action velocity stays below epsilon.
Push actions have agent-target contact evidence.
Throw actions have release or impulse metadata.
Target moves after the action above the expected threshold.
iteration_moves: Add action_trace to expected_physics or runtime artifacts.
Bind the target as a physics-critical rigid body and the agent as an action-producing actor.
Use negative cases for pre-action motion, missing action trace, and no post-action response.

evidence.text: Agent-to-rigidbody coupling requires action trace, target rigid-body evidence, and post-action trajectory response.

capabilities.title: Distance Constraint Motion Validation
capabilities.description: Validate fixed-distance or joint-constrained rigid-body motion with anchor/body trajectory, constraint length labels, constraint trace, and continuity checks. Pendulum swing is only the smoke family.
prompt_moves: Identify the constraint anchor, constrained body, constraint length, tolerance, and expected release or drive condition.
Keep constraint length as a structured physics label rather than a visual rope description.
Use the same capability for pendulums, rope/chain constraints, distance joints, and fixed-length articulated bodies.
verifier_checks: Constraint length label is present and positive.
Anchor and constrained body appear in every trajectory frame.
Measured anchor-body distance stays within tolerance.
Constrained body motion is continuous and does not teleport.
Pendulum-style smoke cases cross or approach the center line after release.
iteration_moves: Export constraint_trace before tuning visual rope or chain assets.
Check constraint_length_m and measured_distance_m before adjusting solver settings.
Add negative cases for length drift, teleporting body, and missing constraint label.

evidence.text: Fixed-distance constraint motion is verified by anchor/body trajectory, constraint labels, and continuity checks.

capabilities.title: Constrained Impulse Chain Transfer
capabilities.description: Validate ordered contact-driven impulse and momentum transfer through a chain of constrained rigid bodies. Newton's cradle is only the smoke family.
prompt_moves: Identify chain_objects, active driver, terminal receiver, adjacent contact chain, mass labels, and expected receiver response.
Keep passive chain members still before their causal contact.
Use contact events and constraint_trace evidence instead of visually keyframing the receiver.
verifier_checks: Chain has at least three bodies.
Passive chain members start below velocity epsilon.
Adjacent contacts exist and are ordered.
Terminal receiver speed exceeds the expected threshold.
Intermediate displacement and energy gain stay bounded.
iteration_moves: Export adjacent contact events before tuning restitution or visuals.
Check passive initial velocity and chain contact order before interpreting receiver motion.
Add negative cases for passive pre-chain motion, terminal no-response, and out-of-order contacts.

evidence.text: Constrained impulse-chain transfer verifies ordered adjacent contacts, receiver response, and bounded intermediate motion.

iteration_playbook.input: README/docs, tests, runtime code, and public config
iteration_playbook.rule: Use WHAT/HOW/FLOW/BRIDGE extraction; do not publish raw sessions.
Remove private paths, secrets, and raw logs; keep owner files and checks.
Run several prompts and classify failures by stage before changing code.

iteration_playbook.output: stage-specific prompt, asset, runtime, verifier, or benchmark patch
iteration_playbook.rule: Change one responsible stage at a time and rerun tests.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Compile object graph
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Create active driver or impactor bodies and passive receiver bodies with stable ids.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Bind physical assets
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Use colliders, mass, rigid body, material, collision profile, and no-overlap semantics for every physics-critical object.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Set physics controls
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Active velocity or impulse encodes requested action; passive bodies start below velocity epsilon.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Execute runtime
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Runtime, not the LLM or keyframes, produces passive motion through contact events.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Verify causality
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Reject if passive bodies move above threshold before first causal contact.

contact_causality_reference_workflow.name: Iterate controls
contact_causality_reference_workflow.contract: Tune speed, restitution, friction, spacing, mass ratio, and solver substeps only after causality passes.
